step_by_step_thinking:
Both zinc oxide eugenol cement and polycarboxylate cement do not involve direct chemical bonding to the tooth structure via polyacrylic acid. Therefore:
\boxed{B} Based on the information given about zinc oxide eugenol cement and polycarboxylate cement, there is no mention suggesting they directly employ chemical bonding mechanisms akin to those seen in glass ionomer cements mediated by polyacrylic acid reacting with calcium ions from the tooth structure. Instead, these other types of cements rely primarily on mechanical interlocking rather than chemical adhesion. Thus, option B stands out as being consistent across all mentioned characteristics for these two specific cements.
